pi_items.verified_value: SOC 15-2011 Actuaries - Property & Casualty Actuaries perform loss reserve analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: NAIC Schedule P Workers Compensation cumulative paid loss triangle, AY 2019-2023; development factors consistent with NAIC Annual Statistical Bulletin 2023 WC industry aggregates; URL: https://content.naic.org/sites/default/files/statistical-studies-annual-statistical-bulletin.htm
pi_items.verified_value: Representative/synthetic workers compensation loss triangle constructed to match NAIC Schedule P format and industry development factor ranges. Data is not directly from NAIC; it is synthetic data modeled on NAIC patterns.
pi_items.source: Task README.md explicitly states data is 'Representative workers compensation program constructed using NAIC Schedule P industry aggregate development patterns'. URL provided returns 404 error.
Direct calculation from workers_comp_triangle.xlsx data: 59650/56840 (only AY 2019 has 60M value)
Calculated from IBNR for each AY: AY 2019 ($1,491K) + AY 2020 ($4,624K) + AY 2021 ($10,041K) + AY 2022 ($20,591K) + AY 2023 ($38,883K) using exact CDF calculations from verified LDFs
AY 2022: IBNR % = 26.7% (exceeds 20% threshold); AY 2023: IBNR % = 48.5% (exceeds 20% threshold). AYs 2019-2021 all below 20% threshold.

pi_items.verified_value: URL returns HTTP 404 error; resource not found

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic/representative workers compensation loss reserve task modeled on NAIC Schedule P format with 5 accident years (2019-2023) and 5 development periods (12-60 months). All calculated metrics are precisely matched to expected values: VW LDFs of 1.4222 (12-24m), 1.1690 (24-36m), 1.0851 (36-48m), 1.0494 (48-60m); total IBNR reserve of $75,630.7K; AY 2023 CDF-to-ultimate of 1.9406; and two under-reserved accident years (2022 and 2023, each exceeding the 20% IBNR-to-ultimate threshold). The occupation (P&C Actuaries, SOC 15-2011) is correctly characterized. The dataset metadata claims the data comes from NAIC Annual Statistical Bulletin 2023, but the provided URL returns 404 and the README clarifies the data is actually synthetic/representative, constructed to be consistent with published NAIC development factor ranges.
